observe: Every request costs the head a trip. The order you choose decides how far it has to travel — and how much of this it survives.
fault: You just watched the head sprint back and forth for no reason. That's what happens without a smarter order.
repair: Direct it properly. Watch the travel distance actually shrink.
escape: Every unnecessary trip out here costs something real. Choose the order carefully.

observe: Disk scheduling algorithms determine the order in which I/O requests are serviced.
fault: FCFS servicing order is causing excessive head travel.
repair: Reorder the request queue to reduce total seek distance.
escape: Confirm the reordered schedule before time expires.

hints: Sorting requests by cylinder distance from the current head position reduces total travel.
